body.notes: Participation in IETF meetings constitutes agreement to the Note Well, which permits recording, transcription, and public archival of proceedings. See also BCP 78 (RFC 5378) and BCP 79 (RFC 8179) for IPR policies.

body.title: SVTA Update to MOPS at IETF 119

the UK also sky so you know we uh cover a lot of video and we cover a lot of networking in my day job and I'm also part of the streaming video technology Alliance where I chair the networking and transport working group over there and I'm the technical adviser to mops and you know mops being an operations group one of the things we try to do here is to make sure that industry is connected back into the ITF and vice versa so while I come over here and talk often about SV ta updates or sometimes uh sanj Mishra uh will also come and talk about these things who does cdni stuff we also go over there when we talk about to those guys what's going on in the ITF to keep everybody in sync so highlights from 2023 uh svj uh did a lot of things uh we put on every year a a big segments conference uh in fact that just happened if you missed it it was in Denver in um uh February and we did it back toback this year with Mile High video and so uh it worked really well it's a very good conference a lot of good participation segments is an external conference so if you want to talk about video and stuff like that uh look for segments 2025 other words going to be yet but it'll probably be in the springtime and may or may not be Co okay with M High video we'll see but it's part of the ongoing operations are reached so segments is operationally focused on video so a lot of ways to think about what svta does is we're sort of like the nanog or an operations uh group that takes work from place like s ITF converts into operational stuff and puts it out there for people to deploy run services on then we collect feedback we either deal with in there or we bring it back to the ITF and we do a lot of documents uh We've also updated our document numbering scheme so that when you're now referring to Us online you won't have to have a URL that's got 5 million characters uh it started out like a really simple thing like hey just create a numbering scheme for documents and as we all know from the ITF that isn't so simple so let me talk to you then about the big mey thing for today uh this is a thing that has been discussed uh at the uh svta we had the a meeting uh the week before ITF did Prague we were svta met in Prague as well uh we started a conversation where people started saying we're finding operational impacts that are being caused by these overlay Networks an example of an overlay Network would be apple private relay uh it's things that it's doing are causing some operational impacts to cdns and video Service delivery and and we're not trying to say that's a terrible service we're trying to say there's some technical choices made there that impact some other technical choices made on the other place and I'm going to go through a little bit of that today and give you a taste uh for the purpose of it what the svca is doing is we're having a discussion uh within that group about trying to catalog in and capture in a document all the various impacts and as I go through these you'll get a taste of some of them we're trying to capture that so that ultimately we're going to come back either to the ITF probably for Vancouver and say Here's a new document with some operational concerns we have could we please engage with everybody here to talk through them and find out if there's new things we can do uh new specifications changes we can make or other things uh either nvta or ITF or both uh to make everybody work really nicely together so at the heart of all the the the the conflict or the problems we're starting to see is that policies uh are changed on network overlays right so I'll show you a picture in a minute of the kind of policy we're impacting but overall it's the selection and the changing out of policies whether it's a routing policy change or a DNS policy change where we swap out the DNS or other things and ironically that fact we're up against uh for talking about this against a another B that's talking about secure network policy uh distribution although on a slightly different topic in a slightly different way we you know we're both talking about Network policies and their impact of video delivery services and how we can make the applications and the video delivery service aware and understanding of the network policies and the changes that come along with them in particular when Network overlays are are present in the delivery path and so you know as I said routing changes are things that are starting pop up and cause trouble uh protocol changes uh where we you know transfer uh if you say I'm you used to be coming for htb2 I'm going to convert you to htb3 and maybe fix you up later on that can cause some problems uh uh DNS changes where we you know you're using DNS uh for directing clients successfully to better cash selection and you suddenly come through an overlay and that information gets lost that becomes a problem and then address changes uh if you go look at there's spec on that's being jointly developed between svta and CTA on tokens for tracking and managing sessions across different CDN instances in that spec they use a Geo hased IP address as an identifier which works really well when you're just on the regular internet but now if I've come through a overlay Network that has change the address space for those clients and then itself is also using a hashed IP address mapping we start getting some conflicts the kind of conflicts we might have seen years ago uh when we were going through double knats so same old stuff just different technology uh Glenn do you want to take comments sure Bob quick question you you should be using V6 example addresses too for your DNS not just V4 they're hard to th on the slide but I agree with you it's a good comment Bob as you know I'm I'm a strong supporter of V6 uh but they hard to fit out on slide uh so anyways here is a very simple example of the kind of overlay scenario we're talking about uh in this case we have an application uh protocol HTTP that is being routed through a different routing policy than all of the other protocols on that same network from the perspective of the client this is causing some obvious cash selectum problems potentially it also causes problems where uh and this is increasingly true in the professional media space we care a lot about latency we are spending lots of time and energy right now figuring ways to measure latency and it's not just end to end latency it's end to end latency plus anything in between that suddenly causes latency to go up so imagine you're watching a massive U sporting event like uh there is an NFL game that was streamed on peacock in the United States recently we really care about the latency from all the clients to the CDN servers that are delivering that content when things get in the way that's a problem and when our tools for measuring latency no longer follow the path that the application will be taking for the video service we have a problem our tools are broken they don't and in this case you'll follow the golden line there that's the flow that the video traffic will you take if it's on a uh this particular overlay network but if you notice up top icmp and Trace rout and other things like that follow with completely different path so very common latency measurement tools no longer work in this workflow that's a problem I don't have a solution today by the way I'm just I'm just up here telling you guys all the problems we're finding the other problem we have too is very often these Network overlays are invisible to the application they will turn on and the application doesn't get notified oh you're in this environment now a lot of people say well that's not really a problem well think about from the application standpoint if I'm an application developer I want know what path my content is going to take well I may use icmp or I may use a ping right with icmp again may do a trace for I may do other things to find that path and they're going to say oh you're going this way when you actually start doing the video float you're going to go on a different path this is again an example of the tools aren't keeping up with sometimes some of the more advanced networking choices we're making and here is uh the cache selection problem highlighted so on the uh bottom uh Corner the bottom left hand side you have two versions of the same application one is doing video streaming uh using HTTP and one is using video streaming using https uh if I'm in an environment which only flows HTTP over an overlay then I'm going to follow that red line and you see I'm going to go up I'm going to get ingested this is a mask relay that I'm illustrating here it goes up get ingested gets unwrapped comes back down and I'm going to hit the the transit is going to eventually go on the open transit and I'm going to get the DNS or sorry the the CDN cache that's on the open internet same video service also has a video cache on the access network that's really what I want to hear because it's G to be the fast one right the the Blue Line the blue flow where if I'm doing htps htps in this particular overlay which is Apple private relays behavior is not going to flow up into the Apple framework it's going to flow into the uh Network it's going to say oh well you've got a cash right here and it's going to connect me on the cach so here's a difference in behavior on if the URL contains HTTP or https I get very different behaviors of what caches ultimately get selected and that could be for a couple reasons it could be a runting policy change it could be that the DNS in the overlay network doesn't support edns zero if my application and my workflows are making use of edns zero for cash selection a lot of the like Cloud FL for instance doesn't support edns zero information uh for cach Direction so that that minor change has a big change implication to the video flow and and the delivery path and likewise even at the application Level it doesn't know what's going on and so if it tries to test and tries to figure out it doesn't always get the right answer so this then causes the problem of what cash do I get and what are my what's my latency what's my performance to it now a lot of people say well we use these overlays for for uh privacy reasons and that's a good cause in this case though it is the same user the same account ultimately going to the same video cache to a service that it actually has a login with right so the cach on each side knows who it's talking to so other than the the network pass taken you get the exact same result and on the bottom it's you know htps and it's quick so quick is doing the security and the Privacy part right there so if you're on the network you're trying to poke in and see what the guy's watching you can't quick is already taken care of that so it's not a big privacy issue necessarily but it is a performance and is a latency issue any questions on this anybody want to come up and tell me I'm wrong because we're seeing this in in the wild and it's causing some real problems so next steps what are we going to do about we found a problem we found a series of problems we're going to document them we're going to tease them out put them in a document so that people can understand them so we can have a uh grown up engineer to engineer conversation about what the impacts are there's a partial list of the stuff we've already identified that is a problem problem there's probably going to be more as we tease it out but then we're going to come back to the ITF and say here's some problems so stay tuned mops 120 I'll be back and we're going to be probably doing some IDs and and trying to get some engagement with other parts of the ITF on this very real problem that the operational Community for uh professional streaming media is encountering on a daily basis and with that that's all I got any any questions any this is the ITF I'm I'm used to getting yelled at so somebody needs to get up to a microphone and yell at me about something I got wrong any any questions about what Glennon has presented Andor any thoughts about other areas that should be explored in this vein hi so I think you don't care much about ICN but I believe ICN address everything yes this is a a nonic environment it's primarily an HTTP streamed um using manifests uh is the delivery path that we're worried about here okay so you don't care so much about the different approach so you definitely want to use correct okay we we have hundreds of millions of clients deployed already uh a whole industry based around this current technology flow and I know ICN does offer some uh unique features but the cats out of the bag now they're firmly entrenched in HTP as a delivery path um just a side note uh thank you Eric and George for using the Q tool so people who would like to um it not a problem but just going going forward anybody would like to speak please do put yourself in queue thanks okay every the responsibility uh for mops I think this document should at least be presented in mops I say at least because maybe it should be presented in other areas as well this is about media operation like clearly and we know each other in the ATF so we will make sure that we announce this draft as well to the relevant people and I forgot to mention one thing in that is a lot of the people in room and probably work for organizations that are members of the svta and so if you'd like to help write the document over there your help would be very welcome too come see me afterwards and if you're not sure if you're a member look on the sb.org website and if you want to become a member I can make that happen too George Michaelson apick so I really like this talk and I think that you are talking about real problems but I think that this will be a crosswork group conversation because this is almost identical to things that arose naturally with IP and IP of any kind so any tunnel exposing protocol any overlay fabric vpns Global VPN selection of CDN under VPN termination address how do you expose the true address ECS client subnet marking in DNS it goes into so many working groups that I don't think it's going to have a simple home and purely from my own personal perspective and in no sense to say don't do this I think it will be extremely hard to terminate this and the primary reason is because nobody actually knows how to do talk about policy in a way which rationally terminates because you need languages and meta languages to describe the Infinity of policy capabilities but you can't actually nail them down to the small set that rationally suit the use case so I do think this is good work and I really like your presentation but dot dot dot I and I think you're right I think the challenge here is not to boil the ocean and that's why narrowing it down to focus on a couple of the very specific things such as CD and cash selection and that space is probably the easy solution path here because there is so much here uh you know and and I want to be clear we're not trying to undo the Privacy initiatives that the ITF has spent the last what 10 years developing we're just trying to make sure that the workflows for other stuff work with them so that's it thank you no no Warren you're in you tricked me you weren't at the microphone War I actually dropped out of the queue but I just kind of respond to like something that George had said yes this is really nice because it's operational and it kind of fits exactly within the idea of this group like you know these are the sort of issues we're working on blah blah blah so good work thank you thank you and now am I really done and now I believe you're really done um in fact I believe we are all really done uh any other business that people want to raise going once going twice all right um thank you all and those who are staying for add I think you have a few minutes of rest and the rest of us are going to Scurry over to scone Pro all right thank you you you have 38 minutes to get a coffee or go to scone Pro but be back here for 10:30 because we're gonna have some great conversations and we stay the [Music] no that far 10 a deeply envious deeply deeply in this no no Ben's gonna stay for his working group better he right there yep not yet
